project.name: taskflow-api
project.description: A task management REST API built with Express and Prisma. Supports user authentication, project workspaces, task CRUD with assignments, and real-time notifications via WebSocket. Designed for team collaboration with role-based access control.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Application entry point. Initializes the Express server, applies middleware, mounts route handlers, and starts listening on the configured port.

nodes.name: task.routes.ts
nodes.summary: Defines REST endpoints for task CRUD operations: GET /tasks, POST /tasks, PATCH /tasks/:id, DELETE /tasks/:id. Validates request bodies with Zod schemas before delegating to TaskService.

nodes.name: auth.routes.ts
nodes.summary: Authentication routes: POST /auth/login, POST /auth/register, POST /auth/refresh. Handles credential validation and JWT token issuance.

nodes.name: project.routes.ts
nodes.summary: Project workspace endpoints: CRUD for projects plus member management. Enforces role-based access so only admins can modify project settings.

nodes.name: task.service.ts
nodes.summary: Business logic for tasks. Handles creation, status transitions, assignment, due-date validation, and triggers notifications on state changes.

nodes.name: createTask
nodes.summary: Creates a new task in the database. Validates the assignee belongs to the project, sets default status to 'todo', and publishes a TASK_CREATED event to the notification service.

nodes.name: transitionStatus
nodes.summary: Moves a task through its lifecycle (todo -> in_progress -> review -> done). Enforces valid transitions and records the state change timestamp.

nodes.name: auth.service.ts
nodes.summary: Authentication service handling password hashing with bcrypt, JWT token generation/verification, and refresh token rotation.

nodes.name: notification.service.ts
nodes.summary: Manages real-time notifications via WebSocket. Subscribes to domain events (task created, assigned, status changed) and broadcasts to relevant connected clients.

nodes.name: Task
nodes.summary: Prisma model wrapper for tasks. Defines the schema fields (title, description, status, priority, dueDate, assigneeId, projectId) and provides static query helpers.

nodes.name: User
nodes.summary: Prisma model wrapper for users. Stores email, hashed password, display name, and role. Provides methods for credential verification and profile serialization.

nodes.name: prisma.ts
nodes.summary: Singleton Prisma client instance. Configures connection pooling, logging levels, and graceful shutdown hooks.

nodes.name: auth.middleware.ts
nodes.summary: Express middleware that extracts and verifies JWT from the Authorization header. Attaches the decoded user payload to req.user for downstream handlers.

nodes.name: requireRole
nodes.summary: Higher-order middleware factory that checks if the authenticated user has one of the required roles. Returns 403 Forbidden if the role check fails.

nodes.name: validate.ts
nodes.summary: Shared Zod schemas and a validateBody middleware factory. Schemas cover task creation, user registration, and project settings validation.

nodes.name: logger.ts
nodes.summary: Structured logging utility built on pino. Provides log levels (debug, info, warn, error) and request-scoped child loggers with correlation IDs.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Loads environment variables and exports typed configuration: port, database URL, JWT secret, token expiry, log level, and WebSocket settings.

nodes.name: routes
nodes.summary: Route module aggregating all HTTP endpoint definitions. Each route file maps URL paths to controller logic with middleware chains.

nodes.name: Authentication Flow
nodes.summary: The system uses JWT-based stateless authentication. Users log in with email/password, receive an access token (15 min) and refresh token (7 days). The auth middleware validates tokens on protected routes, and the refresh endpoint rotates tokens to maintain sessions.

nodes.name: Task Lifecycle
nodes.summary: Tasks follow a state machine: todo -> in_progress -> review -> done. Only valid forward transitions are allowed. Each transition triggers a notification event and records a timestamp. Tasks can also be archived after completion.

edges.description: Mounts task routes at /api/tasks
Mounts auth routes at /api/auth
Mounts project routes at /api/projects
Reads server port and startup configuration
Applies JWT auth middleware globally to protected routes
Delegates task operations to the service layer
Uses Zod schemas to validate incoming task payloads
Delegates login, register, and token refresh to auth service
Requires authentication and role checking on all endpoints
Queries and mutates task records via the Task model
Emits TASK_CREATED and STATUS_CHANGED events to the notification service
Looks up users for authentication and creates new user records
Reads JWT secret and token expiry from configuration
Logs WebSocket connection events and notification delivery
Uses the shared Prisma client for all database queries
Uses the shared Prisma client for user queries
Auth service implements the core authentication flow
Auth middleware enforces the authentication flow on routes
The transitionStatus function implements the task state machine
Lifecycle transitions trigger real-time notifications
Validates task data before persisting

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: HTTP route handlers and request/response processing. The entry point for all client interactions with the system.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Core business logic and domain operations. Orchestrates data access, validation, event publishing, and enforces business rules.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Database models, Prisma client, and data access patterns. Provides the persistence foundation for all domain entities.

layers.name: Middleware Layer
layers.description: Cross-cutting concerns applied to request pipelines: authentication, authorization, and request validation.

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: Configuration, logging, and shared utilities that support all other layers without containing business logic.

tour.title: Where It All Begins
tour.description: The application starts in index.ts, which wires up Express middleware and mounts all the route handlers. Think of it as the front door of the API.

tour.title: Handling Requests
tour.description: Incoming HTTP requests hit the route files. Each route file defines endpoints for a domain area (tasks, auth, projects) and validates input before passing it along.

tour.title: Security Gate
tour.description: Before most routes run, the auth middleware checks the JWT token. The requireRole function adds role-based access control on top of basic authentication.
tour.languageLesson: JWT (JSON Web Token) is a compact token format that encodes user identity and permissions. It lets the server verify who you are without storing session data.

tour.title: Business Logic
tour.description: Services contain the real brains of the application. TaskService manages task creation and status transitions, while AuthService handles password hashing and token management.

tour.title: Data Persistence
tour.description: The Task and User models wrap Prisma queries. They talk to the database through a shared Prisma client configured in prisma.ts.
tour.languageLesson: Prisma is an ORM (Object-Relational Mapper) that lets you write database queries in TypeScript instead of raw SQL. It auto-generates types from your schema.

tour.title: Real-Time Updates
tour.description: When tasks change state, the notification service broadcasts updates over WebSocket so connected clients see changes immediately without polling.
